# Typeform Schema Extractor

> An open-source tool and npm library by Roope Ripatti for extracting, visualizing, and exporting the full structure of any Typeform — every question, logic branch, and condition — so you can see the complete picture at once. Licensed under MIT.

Typeform's one-question-at-a-time UI is great for short surveys but painful when you need to prepare thoughtful answers for a long application — you can't see what's coming, you can't draft responses in a separate document, and going back to review is tedious. This tool solves that by extracting the complete form structure and laying it all out at once.

The project has two parts:

1. **Website** — a web app where you paste any public Typeform URL and instantly get an interactive flow graph showing every question and conditional branch, plus one-click export as a Markdown table, Mermaid flowchart, or SVG.
2. **npm package** (`typeform-extractor`) — the underlying TypeScript library that does the heavy lifting: fetches the embedded Typeform schema, builds a directed graph, and renders it in multiple formats. Zero runtime dependencies — use it in your own scripts or tools.

How it works:

- Every public Typeform URL (e.g. `https://form.typeform.com/to/XXXXX`) embeds a JSON schema in its HTML. The extractor fetches that HTML, parses out the schema, and returns a `TypeformSchema` object containing all fields, logic rules, and thank-you screens.
- `buildGraph(schema)` converts the flat schema into a `FormGraph` — a directed graph with nodes (questions, endings) and edges (sequential flow, conditional branches, default jumps).
- Renderers turn the graph into the output format you need:
  - **Markdown table** — every field listed with its type, title, and choices; ideal for drafting answers in any text editor
  - **Mermaid flowchart** — a flowchart source you can embed in docs, Notion, or GitHub READMEs
  - **Interactive React Flow diagram** — a zoomable, pannable graph in the browser
  - **SVG** — a rendered vector image of the flowchart (generated via Kroki)

Use cases:

- Preparing answers for a Typeform application by viewing all questions upfront
- Documenting a form's structure and branching logic for a team
- Auditing conditional logic in complex multi-branch forms
- Embedding form flow diagrams in documentation or presentations

## Docs

- [npm package README](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/README.md): Library API overview with install instructions, project structure, and usage examples
- [Website README](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/website/README.md): Dev setup, scripts, routes, and project structure for the web app

## API

- [Type definitions](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/types.ts): All TypeScript types — TypeformSchema, TypeformField, LogicEntry, FormGraph, GraphNode, GraphEdge, etc.
- [Public exports](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/index.ts): Barrel export listing every public function and type
- [Extractor](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/extractor.ts): `extractFormJson` and `getTypeformSchema` — HTML fetching and schema parsing
- [Graph builder](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/graph.ts): `buildGraph` — converts TypeformSchema to FormGraph
- [Mermaid renderer](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/renderers/mermaid.ts): `toMermaidSource`, `saveMermaidMd`, `saveMermaidSvg`, `getMermaidSvg`
- [Markdown table renderer](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/renderers/markdown-table.ts): `toMarkdownTable`, `saveMarkdownTable`
- [React Flow renderer](https://github.com/ripattir/typeform-question-extractor/blob/main/typeform-schema-extractor/src/renderers/react-flow.ts): `saveReactFlowHtml`

## Website

- [Home page](https://typeform-extractor.com/): Main extractor UI — paste a Typeform URL, see the interactive graph, and export
- [About page](https://typeform-extractor.com/about): Project motivation, features, and links
